site_analysis_review.anti_bot_correction: Site analysis claims 'detected: false, severity: low'. In reality, Amazon DOES block direct HTTP with CAPTCHA challenges ('opfcaptcha.amazon.com'). The HTTP fetch returns a 'Click the button below to continue shopping' page. However, a real browser (Playwright) can bypass this without visible CAPTCHA, which is why the site_analyzer reported no anti-bot — it worked at browser level but anti-bot IS present at HTTP level.
site_analysis_review.mechanism_justification: Confirmed: Playwright (browser) is required. Direct HTTP requests return CAPTCHA challenge pages. All product data loads in browser-rendered HTML. JS rendering is mandatory.
corrections: Anti-bot severity should be 'medium', not 'low'. Amazon actively blocks direct HTTP with CAPTCHA (confirmed via web_fetch). Browser-level access works, but anti-bot IS present.

connectivity.notes: Direct HTTP returns CAPTCHA challenge (opfcaptcha.amazon.com). Playwright browser without proxy successfully loads product pages. If CAPTCHA is encountered during scraping, escalate to datacenter or residential proxy. Rate limiting: 3s+ delay between requests recommended.

extraction_methods.notes: Amazon product pages typically have no JSON-LD structured data. All extraction must be done via CSS selectors on the rendered DOM. Amazon's DOM is well-documented and stable — selectors have been consistent for years.

url.notes: Use the clean URL without query parameters. ASIN-based URL pattern.

src_url.notes: Set from input_urls.json by the scraper.

status_code.notes: Set by the scraper from HTTP response.

scraped_at.notes: Set by the scraper as ISO-8601 timestamp.

remarks.notes: Set by the scraper for any warnings or notes.

title.notes: The #productTitle span contains the full product title, usually with leading/trailing whitespace that needs trimming.

price.notes: Amazon uses multiple price IDs depending on product type. .a-price .a-offscreen is the most universal for current items. For Deal/Deal-of-Day items, #priceblock_dealprice is used. Price inside .a-offscreen is a screen-reader-only span containing the formatted price string. Some products show price range (e.g., '$19.99 - $29.99'). For unavailable/out-of-stock products, no price element exists.

original_price.notes: The strikethrough/compare-at price appears as a separate .a-price element with class 'a-text-price'. Only present when item is on sale/discounted.

currency.notes: Derived from price string prefix (e.g., '$' for USD, '€' for EUR, '£' for GBP, '¥' for JPY). Default to 'USD' for amazon.com domain.

examples: Only 5 left in stock - order soon.
In Stock (Ships from and sold by Amazon.com)
availability.notes: Availability text varies. Look for key phrases: 'In Stock', 'Only X left', 'Currently unavailable', 'Out of Stock', 'Temporarily out of stock', 'Available from these sellers'.

asin.notes: ASIN is the 10-char alphanumeric ID. Extract from URL (patterns: /dp/{ASIN}, /gp/product/{ASIN}, /product/{ASIN}). Hidden input 'ASIN' often exists in the DOM as well.

image.notes: #landingImage is the main product image. The src attribute contains the full-resolution image URL. Image data also available via JavaScript data attributes: data-old-hires, data-a-dynamic-image.

rating.notes: Rating text format: 'X.Y out of 5 stars'. Parse numeric value with regex. Only present if product has reviews.

review_count.notes: Review count text format: 'X,XXX ratings'. Parse with regex to extract number. Only present if product has reviews.

brand.notes: The byline link text is usually 'Brand: {Brand Name}' or 'Visit the {Brand Name} Store'. Parse accordingly.

breadcrumbs.notes: Breadcrumb links provide category hierarchy. Each <a> tag is one breadcrumb level.

category.notes: Derived from breadcrumbs — use the last breadcrumb item as the product category.

description.notes: Two sources: #productDescription (full description text) and #feature-bullets (bullet point features). Bullet points are often more useful/structured.

feature_bullets.notes: Key product features displayed as bullet points. Usually 5-8 bullet items.

sku.notes: SKU on Amazon is typically the ASIN. Available in the product details table.

jsonld_extraction.notes: Amazon product pages typically do not include JSON-LD structured data. All extraction must be done via CSS selectors on the rendered DOM.

variants.details: Amazon products often have variants (size, color, style). Variant data is in the DOM via Twister JavaScript object. The main product title and price update dynamically when a variant is selected.
variants.notes: The scraper should extract data for the default variant (first selected). No need to click through variants. If price shows a range (e.g., '$19.99 - $29.99'), it indicates variants with different prices.

page_structure.notes: Amazon page structure is well-documented and stable. Key content areas are in #centerCol (main content) and #rightCol (buy box/price). The #productTitle, #priceInsideBuyBox_feature_div, and #availability are the most reliable anchors.

anti_bot_handling.captcha_indicator: Title changes to 'Amazon.com' (no product name), presence of 'validateCaptcha' form action, 'Click the button below to continue shopping' text
anti_bot_handling.mitigation: Use Playwright browser for all requests. Maintain 3+ second delays between requests. If CAPTCHA detected, wait and retry or escalate to proxy.
anti_bot_handling.detection_method: Check if #productTitle exists on page. If missing or page title is just 'Amazon.com', CAPTCHA was triggered.

mechanism_reassessment.justification: Confirmed: Playwright is required. Direct HTTP returns CAPTCHA. No JSON-LD available. All extraction via CSS selectors on browser-rendered DOM. Anti-bot severity reassessed to 'medium' (not 'low' as site_analyzer stated).

url_patterns.asin_extraction: Extract from URL path using regex: /(?:\/dp\/|\/gp\/product\/|\/product\/)([A-Z0-9]{10})/i
url_patterns.notes: Input URLs may use any of these patterns. Always extract ASIN from URL. Some URLs include referral tags after /ref=. Strip query params for clean URL storage.

confidence_notes: High confidence on field selectors — Amazon's DOM structure is well-documented and stable. Confidence reduced from 1.0 because Playwright browser was unavailable during this analysis session (connection refused), preventing live DOM verification. Selectors are based on site_analyzer's confirmed Playwright session plus extensive knowledge of Amazon's documented DOM structure.
